publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-08-06 21:43:58 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, photographs, printed material, scrapbooks, diaries, publications.
did.abstract: Collection includes personal correspondence with Dr. Kessler's wife, Jessica Winnick Kessler, 1936-1960, 1942-1945 (bulk); his son Sanford, 1943-1945; and friends and acquaintances, 1918-1967; business correspondence pertaining to the Kessler Foundation, 1952-1958; scrapbooks and loose clippings; programs; miscellany documenting his travels, work and awards, 1928-1963; photographs showing his patients and colleagues, ca.1930-1945; diaries and notebooks, ca. 1947-1949; and copies of a number of Kessler's published works, 1925-1968.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Henry Howard Kessler (Cornell University Class of 1916, M.D. 1919) was an orthopedic surgeon and a medical administrator. He served the United Nations as international consultant on rehabilitation of amputees and people with congenital birth defects. He was a captain in the U.S. Navy, 1941-1945. He founded and directed The Kessler Institute for Rehabilitation, West Orange, New Jersey. He died in 1978.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes personal correspondence with Dr. Kessler's wife, Jessica Winnick Kessler, 1936-1960, 1942-1945 (bulk); his son Sanford, 1943-1945; and friends and acquaintances, 1918-1967; business correspondence pertaining to the Kessler Foundation, 1952-1958; scrapbooks and loose clippings; programs; miscellany documenting his travels, work and awards, 1928-1963; photographs showing his patients and colleagues, ca.1930-1945; diaries and notebooks, ca. 1947-1949; and copies of a number of Kessler's published works, 1925-1968.

emph.ref: Series II. Scrapbooks, Clippings, Programs, Menus, etc.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Henry Howard Kessler. Papers, #3016. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Subseries Ia. Letters to Jessica Winnick Kessler
Subseries Ib. Letters Exchanged Between Sanford Kessler and His Father
Subseries Ic. Correspondence Pertaining to the Kessler Foundation
Series II. Scrapbooks, Clippings, Programs, Menus, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, Primarily Mare Island Naval Hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Menus, Hunterian Society Lectures papers,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: South Pacific; postcards; menus; poem; SPAEF Vol. 1, no. 3; Dec 3 1942 and Aug 16 1942 Medical Officers watch list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous notes on foreign opinion of the United States; famous humanists; Commencement Address at Albertus Magnus College by Charles McKerr Parr; passover sermon, "This is the Pacific."

did.unittitle: Scrapbook A: American Physician's Club Trip to Europe - pp. 1-12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters, programs, tickets, photos, postcards, souvenirs

scopecontent.p: Photos, menus, letters, Comeback magazine, invitations, Cornell University Medical School Alumni Bulletin

scopecontent.p: Letters, clippings, photos, cards

scopecontent.p: Letters, programs, pertaining to the Budapest Conference

scopecontent.p: Clippings, letters, programs

scopecontent.p: Clippings, a reprint, U.S. Naval Institute proceedings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blue scrapbook, clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blue scrapbook, name on cover, article related to Kessler's receipt of the American Design Award (Apr 20, 1944)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drawing at operation before incision; man with paralysis of right arm; boy with crippled arm; crippled arms; 6 yr. old boy; right arm fitted with cineplastic prothesis; stump

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same man with prothesis, stump, stress on stump; hand holding box; brochure for cineplastic artificial arms; man with Kessler; stump

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 photos of man hammering with artificial arms; stump; stump with tattoos, man hammering with artificial ars; man showing interchangeable tools, man with stump, prothesis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo of operation, man painting, sawing; stump, arm showing injuries; prothesis; cineplastic arm for forearm amputation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 application of dorsal motor to prothesis: 2 x-rays of bones, hand, hand mechanism

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arm lifting chain, 2 x-rays of broken arm, 2 stumps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: X-ray of flail elbow, 2 artificial arms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stump with arm, 2 artificial arms, 2 stumps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 stumps, infant with congenital loss of 1 arm; 2 girls with artificial arms, 2 model boats

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boy with cineplastic forearm, 2 men with forearm prothesis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 forearm prothesis; stump at shoulder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 arms with prothesis; 4 stumps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 stumps, young man, with and without legs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: X-ray of legs, 8 stumps, 3 articifical arms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: X-ray of legs, 4 stumps, 4 artificial arms

Kessler With Others and Portrait of a Child
Kessler with other Officers, Patients, Mess, Sailors
Kessler in Formation with Others at Mare Island, with Effigy of Tojo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maroon, Dec 2 - Dec 14 (no year)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black, Aug 6 - Sep 8; Hawaii, Japan, Hong Kong, Thailand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown leather

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Green; account of Kessler's trip to Rome to present an exhibit of United States techniques to the meeting of the International College of Surgeons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deep purple; around the world

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous medical notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Treatment of Pneumonia with Diathermy" (1925), "The Curative Workshop and the Determination of the Capacity to Work" (1936), "Traumatic Appendicitis" (1938), "The Office Management of Low Back Pain" (1932), "Disability Evaluation Following Fracture", "Rehabilitation of the Amputee"

did.unittitle: United Nations Technical Assistance Administration on a Mission to Yugoslavia
"Report of Rehabilitation Mission to the Philippines"
"Report of Rehabilitation of the Handicapped in the Philippines"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Proceedings: Eastern Connecticut Conference on the Rehabilitation of Injured Workers" (1951), "Address to the International Society for the Welfare of Cripples" (1951), "Address given at the Dedication of the New Convalescent Wing of the Michael Reese Hospital" (Dec. 14, 1957), "Address at the First National Vocational Council in Israel" (Dec. 8, 1958), Proceedings of the Texas Employers Insurance Association Rehabilitation Symposium

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: People (Nov. 19, 1952), America (no. 17), United Nations Bulletin (Jan. 15, 1954, Vol. XVI no.2), United Hospital News (Vol. 1 no. 2, July 1959)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell University Medical College Bulletin (Aug 1944), World Over (Jan 25, 1952), New Era (Vol. 1 no. 2 Mar. 1952), Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation (Vol. XXXIV no. 10, Oct. 1953)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Source unknown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The DeWitt Clinton High School Yearbook (1912)
